anti_goals.title: Implement the final production harness
anti_goals.prohibition: The Builder may generate justified scaffolding and test assets but may not silently implement production business or creative logic.

anti_goals.title: Become a universal agent factory
anti_goals.prohibition: The product compiles three explicit CMF target types and must not generalize beyond proven abstractions.

anti_goals.title: Collapse atomic harnesses into a universal creative engine
anti_goals.prohibition: Atomic production promises, visual grammar, sequence policy, and evaluation remain harness-owned unless reuse is proven through implementations.

anti_goals.title: Flatten the four format categories
anti_goals.prohibition: Short-Form Edited Video, 2D Character Animation, Carousels, and Supervisuals retain separate constitutions and runtime laws.

anti_goals.title: Treat editing Formats 01–08 as cosmetic themes
anti_goals.prohibition: Format profiles represent materially different visual, temporal, sonic, state, and sequencing systems.

anti_goals.title: Infer meaning before parsing syntax
anti_goals.prohibition: Observed components and temporal states must precede visual-function and Activative hypotheses.

anti_goals.title: Promote unsupported knowledge
anti_goals.prohibition: Generated or hypothesized values cannot silently become observed, measured, or ratified.

anti_goals.title: Turn every difficult capability into a skill
anti_goals.prohibition: Deterministic modules, typed programs, references, human authority, and independent evaluators must be considered first.

anti_goals.title: Confuse canonical skills and runtime capsules
anti_goals.prohibition: Ephemeral compiled prompts are not automatically reusable canonical capabilities.

anti_goals.title: Ship untested required skills
anti_goals.prohibition: A structurally valid skill without behavioral evaluation cannot support production authorization.

anti_goals.title: Let the model own critical orchestration
anti_goals.prohibition: Phase invocation, dependency resolution, skill selection, reference loading, budgets, validation, and repair routing are deterministic control-plane responsibilities.

anti_goals.title: Use accumulated conversation history as the main interface
anti_goals.prohibition: Authoritative phase handoffs are typed contracts; history is audit material only.

anti_goals.title: Silently truncate required context
anti_goals.prohibition: Budget pressure must block, redesign, retrieve, compress through an approved policy, or receive authorization.

anti_goals.title: Repair outside the responsible layer
anti_goals.prohibition: Repair and invalidation must follow the typed graph and preserve still-supported state.

anti_goals.title: Issue readiness from document completeness
anti_goals.prohibition: Authorization depends on evidence, architecture, skill maturity, benchmark, observability, and human gates.

anti_goals.title: Hide operational state
anti_goals.prohibition: Important transitions, failures, costs, waivers, and actions must be observable and receipted.

anti_goals.title: Claim general readiness from one harness
anti_goals.prohibition: Release 1 proves one path; general certification requires transfer across the agreed portfolio.

anti_goals.title: Duplicate authoritative truth across artifacts
anti_goals.prohibition: Documents and code-generation manifests must compile from canonical IR or registries.

anti_goals.title: Hide a production workflow inside one skill or agent session
anti_goals.prohibition: A production Builder workflow must separate orchestration, deterministic code, bounded agent execution, validation, and human authority into explicit nodes.

anti_goals.title: Use unbounded retry or self-correction loops
anti_goals.prohibition: Every retry, repair, candidate race, and feedback edge requires a limit, terminal state, failure owner, and escalation policy.

anti_goals.title: Parallelize by default
anti_goals.prohibition: Parallel agents and sandboxes are authorized only when dependencies, evaluation, merge semantics, and expected value justify the extra compute and risk.

anti_goals.title: Remove human gates merely to increase automation
anti_goals.prohibition: Human authority may be reduced only after benchmark and operational evidence proves the affected decision class is safe to automate.
